name: Woodlands Academy of the Sacred Heart
affiliation: Roman Catholic (Society of the Sacred Heart)
about: Woodlands Academy of the Sacred Heart is an independent, Roman Catholic, college-preparatory school for young women in grades 9-12, founded in 1858 in Lake Forest, Illinois. It is part of the international Network of Sacred Heart Schools and offers both day and boarding programs on a roughly 41-acre suburban campus. The school provides Advanced Placement and honors coursework along with a strong fine arts and service program.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend a range of colleges including Loyola University Chicago, Marquette University, DePaul University, and other Catholic and national universities
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); North Central Association
notable_alumni: Mary Callahan Erdoes (J.P. Morgan Asset Management CEO), Susan Saint James (actress), Jenny Sanford (former First Lady of South Carolina)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid is awarded through the Clarity platform; merit scholarships include the Head of School Scholarship ($15,000 annually renewable) and the Sr. De La Chapelle STEM Scholarship ($10,000 annually renewable).

name: Culver Academies
affiliation: Independent (non-denominational; includes Culver Military Academy and Culver Girls Academy)
about: Culver Academies is an independent, coeducational boarding school in Culver, Indiana, comprising Culver Military Academy (founded 1894) and Culver Girls Academy (founded 1971). It serves grades 9-12 on an 1,800-acre campus along the shores of Lake Maxinkuckee. The school is fully residential and is known for its leadership program, large endowment, and a student body drawn from across the United States and many countries.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend colleges including Indiana University, Purdue University, University of Pennsylvania, Miami University (Ohio), and other national universities
accreditation: ISACS; TABS; NAIS; AdvancED/Cognia (North Central Association); AMCSUS
notable_alumni: Hal Holbrook (actor), George Steinbrenner (New York Yankees owner), Frank Batten (founder of The Weather Channel), Gene Siskel (film critic)
financial_aid_notes: The school awards over $13 million in need-based aid and over $4 million in merit scholarships annually; about 37% of students receive need-based aid with an average grant near $36,000. Limited need-based aid is available for Canadian and international students.

name: Louisiana School for Math, Science, and the Arts
about: The Louisiana School for Math, Science, and the Arts (LSMSA) is a public, tuition-free, state-funded residential high school in Natchitoches, Louisiana, located on the campus of Northwestern State University. Founded in 1983, it serves high-achieving students from across Louisiana in grades 10-12. Students live on campus and the program is open to qualifying students statewide regardless of family financial need.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend Louisiana State University, Louisiana Tech, Tulane University, Northwestern State University, and selective national universities
accreditation: Cognia (Southern Association of Colleges and Schools); Louisiana Department of Education
notable_alumni: Hong Chau (actress), Rod Dreher (author/journalist), Josh Tickell (filmmaker), Trent Dawson (actor), Angie Drobnic Holan (PolitiFact editor)
financial_aid_notes: There is no tuition to attend. Assistance from the LSMSA Foundation ensures the residential program is available to all qualifying students regardless of family financial need; modest student fees may apply.
extracurriculars: Visual and performing arts (theatre, dance, music, piano, voice)

name: Fryeburg Academy
about: Fryeburg Academy is an independent, coeducational day and boarding school founded in 1792 in Fryeburg, Maine, near the White Mountains. It serves grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year and also functions as the local sending school for several area towns. The boarding program enrolls students from more than 35 countries on a roughly 105-acre campus.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend University of Maine, Penn State, University of California campuses, Boston University, and Colby College, among others
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC); AISNE; NAIS
notable_alumni: David Rohde (journalist), Rufus Porter (founder of Scientific American), Spalding Gray (actor/playwright), Marcus Nash (Olympic Nordic skier)
financial_aid_notes: About 43% of students receive aid, with an average grant near $24,575. The school offers need-based financial aid and merit scholarships, plus a Tuition Lock Program for returning boarding families and special pricing for local New England families.

name: Gould Academy
about: Gould Academy is an independent, coeducational boarding and day school founded in 1836 in Bethel, Maine, in the western Maine mountains. It serves grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year on a 456-acre campus. The school is known for its outdoor and skiing programs, a four-week experiential 'Four Point' term, and a student body that is roughly 30% international.
college_matriculation: Approximately 99% of graduates are accepted to college, attending a range of national liberal arts colleges and universities
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC); TABS; NAIS
notable_alumni: Troy Murphy (Olympic Nordic combined skier), Desiree Plata (MIT environmental engineering professor), Bri Schnorrbusch (US Snowboard Team)
financial_aid_notes: About 40-43% of students receive institutional aid, with an average award near $25,409. Merit scholarships are offered; an international student fee (about $5,222) includes health insurance.
extracurriculars: Competitive ski and snowboard programs (IronwoodFund/Gould Competition Program)

name: Kents Hill School
about: Kents Hill School is an independent, coeducational boarding and day school founded in 1824 in central Maine. It serves grades 9-12 plus a postgraduate year on a 400-acre campus that includes ski and snowboard trails and lake frontage. About a quarter of its students are international, and the school offers an English Language Support program for them.
college_matriculation: About 97% of graduates are accepted to college, attending a range of New England and national colleges and universities
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC)
notable_alumni: Leon Leonwood Bean (founder of L.L.Bean), Alden J. Blethen (publisher of The Seattle Times)
financial_aid_notes: About 60% of students receive financial aid. International students apply for aid through Clarity. A Maine Resident Boarding rate of $56,800 offers an $18,000 discount off the standard boarding tuition.
